title: poststop hook with --restart executed on first restart
body:
### Issue Description

The poststop hook when running with --restart=on-failure is executed on the first failure, not when the container is finally removed (or stopped)

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. setup hook
```
D=$(mktemp -d /tmp/hook.XXXXXX)
cat > "$D/settings.json" <<EOF
{
	"version": "1.0.0",
	"when": { "always": true },
	"hook": {
		"path": "$D/hook.sh"
	},
	"stages": ["prestart", "poststop"]
}
EOF
cat >"$D/hook.sh" <<EOF
#!/bin/sh
exec > "$D/log.\$\$"
cat
EOF
chmod +x "$D/hook.sh"
```

2. Run podman:
```
$ podman run -d --name test_hook --hooks-dir="$D" --restart=on-failure docker.io/alpine sh -c 'date; sleep 1; exit 1'
```

3. wait a few seconds and check logs:
```
$ ls -l "$D"
total 16K
-rwxr-xr-x 1 user grp  47 Mar 27 14:29 hook.sh*
-rw-r--r-- 1 user grp 761 Mar 27 14:30 log.1548105
-rw-r--r-- 1 user grp 481 Mar 27 14:30 log.1548142
-rw-r--r-- 1 user grp 142 Mar 27 14:29 settings.json
$ for f in "$D/log."*; do jq '.status' < "$f"; done
"created"
"stopped"
$ podman ps | grep test_hook
7cbf62011c6e  docker.io/library/alpine:latest  sh -c date; sleep...  11 minutes ago  Up Less than a second ago                        test_hook
$ podman logs test_hook | head
Mon Mar 27 05:30:14 UTC 2023
Mon Mar 27 05:30:15 UTC 2023
Mon Mar 27 05:30:16 UTC 2023
Mon Mar 27 05:30:17 UTC 2023
...
```

### Describe the results you received

post hook was executed once on first restart, then not again. There also does not seem to be any kind of hook that would be executed again on further starts.

### Describe the results you expected

stop hook should not be executed at this point,
OR postcreate hook and post hook should both be re-executed everytime

### podman info output

```yaml

```


### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

affects both rootless/rootfull

tested both debian bookworm (conmon 2.1.6 / runc 1.1.4) and alpine 3.17 (conmon 2.1.7 and runc 1.1.4)

### Additional information

seems consistent.

comments.body: A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Still interested in looking into this, I'll probably have time to look further by the end of May... Unless something else gets priority :| sorry
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

Still interested in fixing this, currently working around the problem by not cleaning up on shutdown which isn't ideal so this is still on my todo...
But unfortunately higher priority work keeps pilling up so still not going to work on this short term; I guess this can stay stale this time and I'll comment again when I finally can take time for this.

Meanwhile if someone else wants to check this out, don't hold back :D


FWIW I wasn't sure if it was a podman/conmon/crun problem but it looks like on podman side alright as the hook seems to be run as part of the podman cleanup command invoked from conmon?
anyway, will look more when time allows, sorry.

Hi!

It's been a while, but apparently somewhere between podman 4.8.3 and 5.0.3, the container netns is now recreated on restart, and our hook depends on the netns (we run `udevfw` to forward udev event to the container) so not having hooks run on restart is now a showstopper... Which means that I can't afford to ignore this anymore.

So, looking into it, our problem seems to be that the `--hooks-dir` option is forgotten between runs: I've added some log in `libpod/container_internal.go` `setupOCIHooks` and it turns out that `c.runtime.config.Engine.HooksDir.Get()` returns the proper option on first run, but restart runs give the default `/usr/share//containers/oci/hooks.d/` directory.

For testing, moving my hooks config to that directory then makes the hook work on every restart, so the problem is really only about the `--hooks-dir` option not persisting, which can then be observed by hooks not being setup (not being listed anymore after first restart in `/home/martinet/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay-containers/*/userdata/config.json` and then obviously not run)


I know next to nothing about the podman code base, but for testing I checked some other setting in that config.json (e.g. `--security-opt=unmask=/proc/acpi` and that's properly persisted, so the hook dir in there is not stored the same as "normal" configs?
```
func persistentPreRunE(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
        if !registry.IsRemote() {
                        podmanConfig.ContainersConf.Engine.HooksDir.Set(podmanConfig.HooksDir)
```

And indeed, looking at persistentPreRunE arguments are quite different:
- first run: `DEBU[0000] Called run.PersistentPreRunE(./podman --log-level=debug run --init -d --name t --replace -ti --restart always --hooks-dir=/tmp/test_hooks --security-opt=unmask=/proc/acpi docker.io/amd64/alpine sleep infinity)`
- restart runs: `May 13 17:47:06 pc-0182 /home/xxx/podman/bin/podman[2174064]: time="2025-05-13T17:47:06+09:00" level=debug msg="Called cleanup.PersistentPreRunE(/home/xxx/podman/bin/podman --root /home/xxx/.local/share/containers/storage --runroot /run/user/1000/containers --log-level debug --cgroup-manager cgroupfs --tmpdir /run/user/1000/libpod/tmp --network-config-dir  --network-backend netavark --volumepath /home/xxx/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes --db-backend boltdb --transient-store=false --runtime crun --storage-driver overlay --events-backend file --syslog container cleanup --stopped-only 86e0ff097dc5766e5764b80ebee66226f7b5e62c3acd74340fb2a8f28b9ca9cf)"`


What's the background behind this separation of configs? Would it make sense to either remember the hooks-dir setting in whatever string is built for PersistentPreRunE args (I'd assume adding it there would make re-init work), or to store the setting elsewhere than ConfainersConf.Engine that might be persisted?

Thanks
